diff:
--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,4 +1,10 @@
+The buggy function for generating a tooltip fragment has a minor issue related to the construction of the return string. The `alt` attribute should ideally contain some descriptive text. If the goal is to make the `alt` attribute meaningful, you might want to include the `toolTipText` there as well, or simply provide a default value. Here's the fixed function:
+
+```java
+// Fixed Function
 public String generateToolTipFragment(String toolTipText) {
-    return " title=\"" + toolTipText
-        + "\" alt=\"\"";
+    return " title=\"" + toolTipText + "\" alt=\"" + toolTipText + "\"";
 }
+```
+
+In this fixed version, the `alt` attribute now contains the same text as the `title`, which makes it more informative. If the `toolTipText` is empty or not suitable for `alt`, you might want to handle that case specifically, depending on the intended use of the function.
